title: onobrychis viciifolia subsp. montana
intro: Onobrychis viciifolia subsp. montana, also known as Alpine Sainfoin, is a subspecies of the Sainfoin plant, a legume native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, which make it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Onobrychis viciifolia subsp. montana include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and act as a natural diuretic. Traditionally, the herbal preparation of this plant is made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
